contentBlocks.original:
भवाय चन्द्रचूडाय निर्गुणाय गुणात्मने ।
कालकालाय रुद्राय नीलग्रीवाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavāya chandrachūḍāya nirguṇāya guṇātmanē ।
kālakālāya rudrāya nīlagrīvāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Bhava (the Existent) — auspiciousness to the moon-crested one (Candracūḍa) — auspiciousness to the attributeless one who is the Self of all qualities — auspiciousness to Kālakāla (the death of Death) — auspiciousness to Rudra — auspiciousness to Nīlagrīva (the blue-throated one).
contentBlocks.meanings: bhavāya: to Bhava (the Existent/Śiva) | candra-cūḍāya: to the moon-crested one | nirguṇāya: to the attributeless one | guṇa-ātmane: to the Self of all qualities | kāla-kālāya: to Kālakāla (Death of Death) | rudrāya: to Rudra | nīla-grīvāya: to the blue-throated one | maṅgalam: auspiciousness

contentBlocks.original:
वृषारूढाय भीमाय व्याघ्रचर्माम्बराय च ।
पशूनाम्पतये तुभ्यं गौरीकान्ताय मङ्गलम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛṣārūḍhāya bhīmāya vyāghracharmāmbarāya cha ।
paśūnāmpatayē tubhyaṃ gaurīkāntāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one mounted on the bull (Vṛṣārūḍha) — auspiciousness to the fearsome one (Bhīma) — auspiciousness to the one robed in tiger-skin — auspiciousness to you, lord of animals (Paśupati) — auspiciousness to the beloved of Gaurī (Gaurīkānta).
contentBlocks.meanings: vṛṣa-ārūḍha: mounted on the bull (Nandī) | bhīma: the fearsome | vyāghra-carma-ambara: robed in tiger-skin | paśūnāṃ pati: lord of animals (Paśupati) | gaurī-kānta: beloved of Gaurī (Pārvatī)

contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one whose body is smeared with sacred ash (bhasma) — auspiciousness to the one wearing the sacred thread of a serpent (nāga-yajñopavīta) — auspiciousness to the one adorned with a rosary of rudrākṣa beads — auspiciousness to Vyomakeśa (sky-haired one, whose hair is the sky).
contentBlocks.meanings: bhasma-uddhūlita-deha: body smeared with sacred ash | nāga-yajñopavītine: wearing the sacred thread of a serpent | rudrākṣa-mālā-bhūṣa: adorned with rudrākṣa rosary | vyoma-keśa: sky-haired one (whose hair is the sky/ether)

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्यचन्द्राग्निनेत्राय नमः कैलासवासिने ।
सच्चिदानन्दरूपाय प्रमथेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryachandrāgninētrāya namaḥ kailāsavāsinē ।
sachchidānandarūpāya pramathēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one whose three eyes are the sun, moon, and fire — salutations to the dweller of Kailāsa — auspiciousness to the one of the nature of existence-consciousness-bliss (sac-cid-ānanda) — auspiciousness to the lord of the Pramathas (Pramatha-īśa).
contentBlocks.meanings: sūrya-candra-agni-netra: whose three eyes are sun, moon, and fire | kailāsa-vāsine: dweller of Kailāsa | sac-cid-ānanda-rūpa: of the nature of existence-consciousness-bliss | pramatha-īśa: lord of the Pramathas (Śiva's attendant hosts)

contentBlocks.original:
मृत्युञ्जयाय साम्बाय सृष्टिस्थित्यन्तकारिणे ।
त्रयम्बकाय शान्ताय त्रिलोकेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛtyuñjayāya sāmbāya sṛṣṭisthityantakāriṇē ।
trayambakāya śāntāya trilōkēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Mṛtyuñjaya (the conqueror of Death) — auspiciousness to Sāṃba (who is together with Ambā/Pārvatī) — auspiciousness to the cause of creation, preservation, and dissolution — auspiciousness to Tryambaka (three-eyed) — auspiciousness to the peaceful one — auspiciousness to the lord of the three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: mṛtyuñjaya: conqueror of death | sāṃba: with Ambā (Śiva with Pārvatī) | sṛṣṭi-sthiti-anta-kāriṇe: cause of creation, preservation, and dissolution | tryambaka: three-eyed | śānta: peaceful | tri-loka-īśa: lord of the three worlds

contentBlocks.original:
गङ्गाधराय सोमाय नमो हरिहरात्मने ।
उग्राय त्रिपुरघ्नाय वामदेवाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaṅgādharāya sōmāya namō hariharātmanē ।
ugrāya tripuraghnāya vāmadēvāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Gaṅgādhara (bearer of Gaṅgā) — auspiciousness to Soma (the moon-bearing one) — salutations to the one who is the essence of both Hari (Viṣṇu) and Hara (Śiva) — auspiciousness to the fierce one (Ugra) — auspiciousness to the destroyer of Tripura — auspiciousness to Vāmadeva.
contentBlocks.meanings: gaṅgādhara: bearer of Gaṅgā | soma: moon-bearing (or having soma, the nectar) | hari-hara-ātman: essence of both Viṣṇu and Śiva | ugra: the fierce one | tripura-ghna: destroyer of Tripura | vāmadeva: the gracious/beautiful god (one of Śiva's five faces)

contentBlocks.original:
सद्योजाताय शर्वाय भव्य ज्ञानप्रदायिने ।
ईशानाय नमस्तुभ्यं पञ्चवक्राय मङ्गलम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadyōjātāya śarvāya bhavya jñānapradāyinē ।
īśānāya namastubhyaṃ pañchavakrāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Sadyojāta (the immediately-born, first face of Śiva) — auspiciousness to Śarva (the archer) — auspiciousness to the bestower of excellent wisdom — salutations and auspiciousness to Īśāna — auspiciousness to Pañcavaktra (the five-faced one).
contentBlocks.meanings: sadyojāta: the immediately-born (first of Śiva's five faces) | śarva: the archer (Śiva who shot Tripura) | bhadra-jñāna-pradāyin: bestower of excellent/auspicious wisdom | īśāna: the ruling one (fifth face of Śiva) | pañca-vaktra: the five-faced one

contentBlocks.original:
सदाशिव स्वरूपाय नमस्तत्पुरुषाय च ।
अघोराय च घोराय महादेवाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadāśiva svarūpāya namastatpuruṣāya cha ।
aghōrāya cha ghōrāya mahādēvāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one of the form of Sadāśiva — salutations to Tatpuruṣa — and auspiciousness to Aghora — and to the fierce one (Ghora) — auspiciousness to Mahādeva.
contentBlocks.meanings: sadāśiva-svarūpa: of the form of Sadāśiva (Eternal Śiva) | tatpuruṣa: That Person (fourth face of Śiva, facing east) | aghora: the not-terrifying (third face, facing south) | ghora: the terrifying/fierce | mahādeva: the Great God

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीचामुण्डाप्रेरितेन रचितं मङ्गलास्पदम् ।
तस्याभीष्टप्रदं शम्भोः यः पठेन्मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīchāmuṇḍāprēritēna rachitaṃ maṅgaḻāspadam ।
tasyābhīṣṭapradaṃ śambhōḥ yaḥ paṭhēnmaṅgaḻāṣṭakam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This auspicious Maṅgalāṣṭakam of Śambhu — inspired by Śrī Cāmuṇḍā — was composed as a place of auspiciousness. Whoever recites it, it grants all wishes to that person.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī cāmuṇḍā-preritena: inspired by Śrī Cāmuṇḍā | racitaṃ maṅgala-āspadaṃ: composed as a place of auspiciousness | tasya abhīṣṭa-pradaṃ: grants all wishes to that person | śambhoḥ: of Śambhu (Śiva) | yaḥ paṭhet maṅgalāṣṭakam: whoever recites the Maṅgalāṣṭakam

contentBlocks.original:
महादेवस्य देवस्य यः पठेन्मङ्गलाष्टकम् ।
सर्वार्थ सिद्धि माप्नोति स सायुज्यं ततः परम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahādēvasya dēvasya yaḥ paṭhēnmaṅgaḻāṣṭakam ।
sarvārtha siddhi māpnōti sa sāyujyaṃ tataḥ param ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites this Maṅgalāṣṭakam of the great god Mahādeva — attains the achievement of all purposes (sarva-artha-siddhi) and thereafter attains identity with him (sāyujya — liberation).
contentBlocks.meanings: mahādeva: the great god | mahādeva-sya devasya: of the divine Mahādeva | yaḥ paṭhet maṅgalāṣṭakam: whoever recites the Maṅgalāṣṭakam | sarva-artha-siddhi: achievement of all purposes | āpnoti: attains | sāyujya: identity/union with the deity (a form of liberation) | tataḥ param: thereafter, beyond that
